step_by_step_thinking:
The Gracey curettes come with various numbers indicating their specific design for different tooth surfaces. Curette number ranges from 1 to 17, each designed for particular teeth or root areas. Numbers 1 through 8 represent buccal (tooth surface next to cheek), while numbers 9 through 17 represent lingual/palatal aspects.

Answer: B To determine which option does not belong among Gracey curettes, we need to consider the numbering system used by these instruments. Options A (#1-2), C (#13-14), and D (#5-16) all fall within recognized numerical sequences that correspond to actual Gracey curettes. Option B (#11-12) also fits into this sequence but might be less commonly referenced compared to others due to its position bridging two distinct sets of designs; however, it still adheres to the established pattern.

Answer: B #11-12 Explanation provided above addresses why
